record.field: Hi.Events is a self-hosted event management and ticketing platform, a privacy-friendly alternative to Eventbrite.

Hi.Events is a self-hosted event management and ticketing platform, a privacy-friendly alternative to Eventbrite.

It lets organizers create event pages, sell tickets and manage attendees end to end, from configuration to check-in at the door. Payments run through your own Stripe account via Stripe Connect, so funds and card data flow straight to your bank without passing through the application. Built on a Laravel (PHP) backend and a React/TypeScript frontend, it targets organizers who want full data ownership and zero per-ticket platform fees.

Key features
  • Multiple ticket types: free, paid, donation and tiered pricing, with per-ticket taxes and fees
  • Customizable event homepages and an embeddable ticket widget for any website
  • QR-code check-in tools and attendee management with multi-user roles and permissions
  • Stripe Connect integration for direct, instant payouts to your own account
  • Custom order forms, attendee messaging and data exports to XLSX or CSV
  • Full REST API and webhooks for integration with Zapier, Make and CRMs
Best for

Event organizers, conferences, concerts, workshops and white-label agencies that need a production-ready ticketing system on their own infrastructure, with complete data control and no vendor lock-in.

services: all-in-one: image: daveearley/hi.events-all-in-one:v0.8.0-beta.1 restart: always environment: - VITE_FRONTEND_URL=https://{{ instance.full_domain }} - APP_FRONTEND_URL=https://{{ instance.full_domain }} - VITE_API_URL_CLIENT=https://{{ instance.full_domain }}/api - VITE_API_URL_SERVER=http://localhost:80/api - VITE_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY - LOG_CHANNEL=stderr - QUEUE_CONNECTION=sync - MAIL_MAILER=array - APP_KEY - JWT_SECRET - FILESYSTEM_PUBLIC_DISK=public - FILESYSTEM_PRIVATE_DISK=local - APP_CDN_URL=https://{{ instance.full_domain }}/storage - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://${POSTGRES_USER}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB} - MAIL_MAILER - MAIL_HOST - MAIL_PORT - MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS - MAIL_FROM_NAME depends_on: - postgres container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network postgres: image: elestio/postgres:16 restart: always environment: - POSTGRES_DB - POSTGRES_USER - POSTGRES_PASSWORD volumes: - ./pg_hi_events_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
